summary.title: A recoverable home edge
summary.boundary: Coordinates lifecycle / does not own runtime or provider
summary.alt: System map showing external inputs feeding a router-primary edge and LAN clients, with independent endpoint fallback and a separate evidence boundary.

architecture.title: Capability-driven architecture
deck: Intent moves down through a thin adapter.
Observed evidence returns on a separate path.
architecture.conclusion: Declared intent is not observed state.
architecture.alt: Architecture diagram with operator intent, lifecycle framework, thin target adapter, runtime and device state, plus an independent observed-evidence return path.

recovery.title: Recovery keeps boundaries distinct
deck: Verified inputs restore the managed path.
Exit and recovery routes remain explicit.
recovery.conclusion: Resume, replace, uninstall, and firmware restore are different operations.
recovery.alt: Recovery diagram separating verified inputs, stable deployment kit, runtime state, previous kit, runtime backup, and independent endpoint fallback.

safety.title: Automation stops at trust boundaries
deck: The routine can observe and prepare.
A human authorizes the consequential step.
safety.conclusion: Failure returns to a named rollback path.
safety.alt: Bounded automation loop from observe and plan through a human authorization boundary to apply and verify, with rollback on failed verification.

evidence.title: Evidence is multidimensional
deck: Each claim needs its own observable signal.
One green dimension cannot substitute for another.
evidence.alt: Evidence matrix separating behavior, health, identity, subscription consumption, topology, release integrity, and boundary verification.
